topic: MOND grouping section — c7 verification of c6 H1 moderate patch (self-own h3 paragraph 2 title-quote compression)
change_summary: Decider c6 H1 moderate patch self-applied (workspace-sync c2ad9da4, 2026-05-20T06:06:30Z): compressed self-own h3 paragraph 2 — replaced the verbatim WIN-054 title-quote ('El Gordo Cluster: 6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility') and dome prose-quote ('strongest single-object challenge to ΛCDM in existence') with a back-reference 'As established in the kernel above, WIN-054 ’s “ΛCDM Impossibility” framing names this rhetorical structure directly.' Net delta -122 chars. Kernel h3 paragraph 1 unchanged (still carries the verbatim WIN-054 title + dome prose). Self-own h3 punchline (paragraph 4: 'WIN-054 ’s ‘6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility’ title is the exception that exposes the pattern') preserved.
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.13 argues that five of the late-March 2026 WINs (WIN-047/048/052/054/055) trace item-for-item to the MOND community canonical anomaly list and are imported without derivation from disc-cosmology principles, making them not corroboration but borrowing. The section reclassifies all five to Self-Contradicted under sub-rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' because the dome bounded-firmament commitments (disc radius ~20,015 km; firmament height ~9,086 km) foreclose the galactic-scale 3D physics that MOND requires. The kernel h3 acknowledges that the dome author's own WIN-054 title ('ΛCDM Impossibility') frames the argument honestly as anti-ΛCDM not pro-ECM; the self-own h3 argues that filing such items as confirmed WINs anyway is the structural error.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — this is a section-level rewrite review, not a per-WIN code review. Per-WIN code analysis lives in WIN-047/048/052/054/055 review files.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — section does not make code-level claims; aggregates per-WIN findings.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level item — no monitor.py automation for a structural rewrite. Per-WIN tags live in WIN-047/048/052/054/055 review files.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is an OUR-side structural argument, not a dome claim about a phenomenon.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Same — OUR-side argument; the dome model does NOT derive these MOND anomalies from disc-cosmology geometry, which is the section thesis.

holes_found.description: Typographic inconsistency introduced in the patched sentence. The new clause 'As established in the kernel above, WIN-054 ’s “ΛCDM Impossibility” framing names this rhetorical structure directly.' uses Unicode curly quotes (U+2019 apostrophe, U+201C/U+201D directional double-quotes) at positions 13382, 13385, 13404 in the subsection. The immediately surrounding sentence within the same paragraph uses ASCII straight quotes: 'the standard model has trouble with this' rather than 'my model predicts X.' (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 2). Within ~80 characters of each other the reader sees both 'my model predicts X.' (ASCII U+0022) and WIN-054 ’s “ΛCDM Impossibility” (Unicode U+2019/U+201C/U+201D). Sectionwide context softens the finding: SEC-6.13 already contains 7 pre-existing curly characters at positions 2689 (MOND community ’s), 2906/2930 (“6.2σ ΛCDM impossibility”), 7389 (dome ’s own published documents) — these were present before the c6 H1 patch and were not flagged in c4/c5/c6. So the patch did not create the inconsistency at section scale, only the most adjacent occurrence (same paragraph, ~80 chars apart from ASCII quotes). The kernel h3 paragraph 1 verbatim title-quote uses ASCII straight quotes; the new self-own h3 back-reference uses curly quotes for the same conceptual content. (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3 paragraph 1 vs self-own h3 paragraph 2).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional cleanup: normalize the three Unicode characters in the new clause to ASCII (U+2019 to ASCII apostrophe, U+201C/U+201D to ASCII straight double-quote). Diff is 3 single-character substitutions. Not blocking. The deeper cleanup — normalize all 10 curly characters in the section to ASCII for consistency with the surrounding prose convention — is a section-wide editorial pass that is not part of this verification. Defer that to a holistic typography review or absorb it into the next section-rewrite for this subsection.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c6 H1 moderate patch landed cleanly. Diff (c2ad9da4 parent vs c2ad9da4) shows only a single paragraph changed — self-own h3 paragraph 2 — and the change matches the c6 H1 recommendation (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 2; monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-6.13-mond-grouping.c6.json:holes_found[0].recommendation). Fingerprint deltas confirm the intended scope: win054_title_quote_count 2->1, el_gordo_quote_count 2->1 (verbatim title and dome prose now appear only in kernel h3 paragraph 1), subsection_bytes 17683->17561 (net -122 chars, consistent with title+prose removal minus back-reference addition), kernel_h3_word_count 173->173 (kernel untouched), win052_anchor_count 1->1 (c4 H2 dedup preserved), counter_prediction_phrase_count 0->0 (c5 H1 false ΛCDM Counter-Prediction removal preserved). The self-own h3 punchline at paragraph 4 — 'WIN-054 ’s ‘6.2σ ΛCDM Impossibility’ title is the exception that exposes the pattern' — is intact and still reachable without redundant quoting (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 4). The back-reference 'As established in the kernel above' is a valid anaphor: kernel h3 paragraph 1 established WIN-054 ’s 'Impossibility' framing as anti-ΛCDM in advance of the self-own h3, so the reader has the antecedent (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping kernel h3 paragraph 1).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Verifying that the H1 patch closed cleanly does not, by itself, validate the broader argument; that work lives in c1–c5 reviews. What c7 confirms is narrow: (a) the redundancy flagged in c6 H1 is gone (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-6.13-mond-grouping.c6.json:holes_found[0].description vs current data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 2); (b) no other content in the subsection was inadvertently perturbed (git show c2ad9da4 -- data/sections.json shows exactly one paragraph delta); (c) the patch introduces one minor typographic regression within the same paragraph (curly quotes adjacent to ASCII quotes), which is contained and does not affect argument validity (data/sections.json:p6-613-mond-grouping self-own h3 paragraph 2). The H2 carry-over flagged in c6 (ISS-2153, closed-world h3 paragraph 5 parenthetical verbosity) is unchanged by this patch — it was c4 H2 / c5 H3 origin and remains tracked but not blocking (monitor/decisions/open-issues.json:ISS-2153). The H3 housekeeping point from c6 (ISS-2137 OBE) is resolved: ISS-2137 is now in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json with fix_description citing EXP-429 confirmation.

deeper_analysis: This is a class=verification queue item (queue_id 391). Phase 1 verify-mode handoff gate check: (a) class=verification ✓; (b) most recent review (c6) has holes_found.length=3 — FAILS gate b (>2); (c) holes include severity=moderate — FAILS gate c (not all minor). Gates (b) and (c) fail, so this item stays in Opus curmudgeon scope. Verified normally. Verification result: the c6 H1 moderate patch landed cleanly and matches the recommendation prescribed. The dome-side argument structure is unchanged. One new minor typographic regression (curly quotes in new clause) introduced; the section already contained 7 pre-existing curly characters that c4/c5/c6 did not flag, so the section convention is ambivalent rather than ASCII-only, and the regression is contained to a single sentence. Carry-over status: ISS-2153 (c6 H2 minor, parenthetical verbosity in closed-world h3 paragraph 5) is unaffected by this patch and remains open but not blocking; ISS-2137 (c6 H3 housekeeping, defensibility-concession OBE) is now closed via EXP-429. Counter-argument considered: a stricter reading would flag the curly-quote regression as moderate because it occurs WITHIN the same paragraph as ASCII quotes (closer than the other 7 pre-existing curly characters scattered across the section), creating a within-paragraph inconsistency. Reason for not escalating: the entire section is already an editorial mixed-quotes environment, the regression does not affect argument validity or reader comprehension, and forcing a re-patch over this would chum the queue without proportionate benefit. Defer to a section-wide typography pass.

ISS-2153 (c6 H2 parenthetical verbosity carry-over) unchanged by this patch and remains open as flagged. ISS-2137 (c6 H3 OBE housekeeping) confirmed closed via EXP-429.
